ddls:
CREATE TABLE "oranges" (
  "orange_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each orange */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the orange (e.g., orange) */,
  "diameter" REAL /* Diameter of the orange */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the orange */,
  "red" INTEGER /* Red color intensity */,
  "green" INTEGER /* Green color intensity */,
  "blue" INTEGER /* Blue color intensity */,
  "origin" TEXT /* Origin of the orange (e.g., farm name, location) */,
  "harvest_date" TEXT /* Date the orange was harvested */,
  "ripeness_level" TEXT /* Level of ripeness (e.g., green, ripe, overripe) */,
  "blemish_level" TEXT /* Level of blemishes (e.g., none, minor, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("orange_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "batches" (
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each batch */,
  "batch_name" TEXT /* Name of the batch */,
  "production_date" TEXT /* Date of production */,
  "producer" TEXT /* Name of the producer */,
  "production_method" TEXT /* Method used for production (e.g., organic, conventional) */,
  "production_conditions" TEXT /* Conditions under which the batch was produced (e.g., weather, soil quality) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orange_batches" (
  "orange_batch_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each orange-batch association */,
  "orange_id" INTEGER /* ID of the orange */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch the orange belongs to */,
  "batch_quantity" INTEGER /* Number of oranges in the batch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("orange_batch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orange_batches_orange_id FOREIGN KEY ("orange_id") REFERENCES oranges ("orange_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orange_batches_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality standard */,
  "attribute" TEXT /* Name of the attribute (e.g., diameter, weight) */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum acceptable value for the attribute */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum acceptable value for the attribute */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the attribute (e.g., cm, kg) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality result */,
  "orange_id" INTEGER /* ID of the orange */,
  "attribute" TEXT /* Name of the attribute checked (e.g., diameter, weight) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the attribute */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the quality check (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  "checker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the quality check */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date the quality check was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_results_orange_id FOREIGN KEY ("orange_id") REFERENCES oranges ("orange_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_results_checker_id FOREIGN KEY ("checker_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the quality result that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date the alert was generated */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES quality_results ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., quality control, administrator) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to (e.g., quality control, production) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_check_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check history entry */,
  "orange_id" INTEGER /* ID of the orange being checked */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date when the quality check was performed */,
  "checker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the quality check */,
  "result" TEXT /* Result of the quality check (e.g., pass, fail) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_check_history_orange_id FOREIGN KEY ("orange_id") REFERENCES oranges ("orange_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_check_history_checker_id FOREIGN KEY ("checker_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "producer_companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each producer company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the producer company */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the producer company */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Contact person for the producer company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "farms" (
  "farm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each farm */,
  "farm_name" TEXT /* Name of the farm */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the farm */,
  "soil_type" TEXT /* Type of soil used on the farm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("farm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "batch_producers" (
  "batch_producer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each batch-producer association */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* ID of the producer company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_producer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batch_producers_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batch_producers_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES producer_companies ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "batch_farms" (
  "batch_farm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each batch-farm association */,
  "batch_id" INTEGER /* ID of the batch */,
  "farm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the farm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("batch_farm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batch_farms_batch_id FOREIGN KEY ("batch_id") REFERENCES batches ("batch_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_batch_farms_farm_id FOREIGN KEY ("farm_id") REFERENCES farms ("farm_id")
)
